semantic.role: Developer guidance for AI coding assistants and human developers interacting with the ArchSpine repository
responsibilities: Topics covered: project overview, CLI commands, strict 6-layer architecture, TypeScript conventions, documentation conventions (English/Chinese mirrors), testing (unit, e2e, schema, CI), MCP server usage for development, and built-in architecture rules.

Maintenance scope: keeping the command table accurate, updating layer model descriptions as new modules are added, ensuring testing and linting commands reflect current tooling, and maintaining the MCP tool list for efficient structural queries.
outOfScope: End-user product documentation or installation guides
Detailed design records or decision log (those live in docs/design/ and docs/planning/)
Architecture planning or temporary design notes (stored in docs/temporary-to-be-cleared/)

English.purpose: Provide clear, actionable guidance to Claude Code and human developers on how to navigate, build, test, and maintain the ArchSpine repository. It emphasises the project's strict 6-layer architecture, TypeScript conventions, and the preferred MCP-based development workflow.
English.context_and_audience: The primary audience is Claude Code (the AI agent) and human developers contributing to the ArchSpine project. Readers are expected to be familiar with TypeScript, the Model Context Protocol (MCP), and the high-level goals of the ArchSpine architectural governance tool.
key_takeaways: ArchSpine enforces a strict 6-layer architecture (CLI, Services, Core/Tasks/Engines, Infrastructure, AST/Types/Utils) with downward-only import dependencies, validated by built-in rules.

All development work should use the MCP server (node dist/cli/index.js mcp start) as the primary interface for querying the semantic index, scanning, and checking architecture rules, instead of running CLI commands directly.

TypeScript conventions include ES modules with explicit .js extensions, strict mode, no require() or 'as any', interface over type for objects, explicit return types on exported functions, kebab-case filenames, and test files co-located or mirrored in tests/.

Documentation is maintained in both English (docs/) and Chinese (docs/zh-CN/) mirrors; VitePress navigation must be updated when adding public docs. Design, planning, and temporary records are excluded from the public nav.
Testing covers unit (60s timeout), e2e (120s timeout), and schema compliance; fixtures live in tests/fixtures/, helpers in tests/helpers/. Use npx vitest run for single test files.
